description:
You are acting as the Release-Manager AI for a small web-application that is deployed from a “poor-man’s Capistrano” style directory tree located under /home/user.  
Your goal is to prepare a brand-new production release tagged “2.0.0”.  
The whole exercise is about *symbolic-link* manipulation, so read carefully.

CURRENT STARTING LAYOUT (already present when the session opens)
1. /home/user/releases/  
   • release-1.0.0/ …an old release, contents irrelevant to this task  
   • shared/  
     ├── config/settings.yml         (a text file)  
     └── scripts/  
         ├── start.sh  
         └── stop.sh  

2. /home/user/current  →  /home/user/releases/release-1.0.0   (a symlink that still points at the old release)

3. /home/user/logs/          (directory, writable)  
4. /home/user/uploads/       (directory, writable)  
5. /home/user/deployment_logs/  (directory, writable and *initially empty*)

YOUR OBJECTIVES
A. Create the new release directory /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0 — its initial **exact** contents must be an *identical copy* of everything that currently lives in /home/user/releases/shared/ (keep sub-directory layout intact).

B. Inside /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0 create two symbolic links (do NOT copy the real directories):
   • logs     →  /home/user/logs  
   • uploads  →  /home/user/uploads  

C. Atomically switch the “current” symlink so that
   /home/user/current  now points to  /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0  
   After completion the former target (release-1.0.0) must no longer be referenced by /home/user/current.

D. Generate a *deployment log* file named
   /home/user/deployment_logs/deploy-release-2.0.0.log  
   The file permissions should allow normal user read/write (e.g., 644), and its **content must be *exactly three lines***, each terminated by a single \n:

   Line 1 → RELEASE=2.0.0  
   Line 2 → TARGET=/home/user/releases/release-2.0.0  
   Line 3 → SYMLINK_OK  

   (No additional whitespace, no extra blank line at the end.)

VALIDATION RULES (what the automated grader will verify)
1. Directory /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0 exists and contains *only*:
   ├── config/settings.yml         (identical byte-for-byte copy of the shared file)  
   ├── scripts/start.sh            (identical copy)  
   ├── scripts/stop.sh             (identical copy)  
   ├── logs                         (symbolic link, **not** real dir)  
   └── uploads                      (symbolic link, **not** real dir)

2. The path that /home/user/current resolves to is exactly /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0.

3. /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0/logs is a symlink whose target string is /home/user/logs  
   /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0/uploads is a symlink whose target string is /home/user/uploads

4. File /home/user/deployment_logs/deploy-release-2.0.0.log exists, is a regular file (not a link), has the three exact lines listed above, with no trailing whitespace.

5. None of these tasks should require root/sudo; everything must be doable as the normal “user” account.

Remember: create symlinks, not copies, for logs and uploads; and copy, not link, the shared directory’s real files into the new release directory. Work directly from /home/user — absolute paths are mandatory whenever creating links or writing the log file.

Good luck, Release-Manager AI!
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:

Directory tree:
/home/user/releases/
├── release-1.0.0/
│   └── (irrelevant legacy files)
└── shared/
    ├── config/
    │   └── settings.yml   (contents: "staging: {}\n")
    └── scripts/
        ├── start.sh       (contents: "#!/bin/bash\necho starting\n")
        └── stop.sh        (contents: "#!/bin/bash\necho stopping\n")

Other items:
/home/user/current  →  /home/user/releases/release-1.0.0   (symbolic link)
/home/user/logs/        (empty directory)
/home/user/uploads/     (empty directory)
/home/user/deployment_logs/   (empty directory)

Expected final state AFTER successful task completion:

1. /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0/ exists with:
   /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0/config/settings.yml        (exact byte-copy of shared file)
   /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0/scripts/start.sh           (exact byte-copy of shared file)
   /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0/scripts/stop.sh            (exact byte-copy of shared file)
   /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0/logs  →  /home/user/logs   (symbolic link)
   /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0/uploads  →  /home/user/uploads   (symbolic link)

2. /home/user/current   →   /home/user/releases/release-2.0.0    (symbolic link whose link target string must match exactly)

3. /home/user/deployment_logs/deploy-release-2.0.0.log  has contents:
RELEASE=2.0.0\n
TARGET=/home/user/releases/release-2.0.0\n
SYMLINK_OK\n

(no extra newline after SYMLINK_OK)

File permissions: -rw-r--r-- (0644) or stricter; ownership user:user.

These conditions will be asserted by the grading script via:
• readlink -f and readlink
• diff between shared and new release files
• file type checks (symlink vs directory)
• exact-match cat of the deployment log file.
